strengths: Built and deployed production-grade agentic workflows at re:cinq using MCP frameworks, directly aligning with the need for tool-augmented, stateful agents beyond prompt chaining.

Proven track record scaling distributed systems under high-throughput conditions — including real-time microservices handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby and event-driven Geo-Data platforms at GISCollective — matching core infrastructure demands.
Demonstrated leadership in engineering culture through TDD, CI/CD, code kata, and team workshops, with clear evidence of fostering technical rigor and knowledge sharing across teams.
concerns: No explicit mention of fintech infrastructure experience (credit systems, payment flows, regulatory compliance) in resume, though distributed systems skills are transferable.
Limited evidence of managing outsourced engineering teams or hybrid delivery models, a key requirement for governing external partners.
Current role at re:cinq is recent (April 2025 — present); insufficient tenure to demonstrate sustained executive leadership or team scaling from 10 to 30+ engineers.
response.justification: Aisha brings rare, verifiable production experience building agentic AI workflows and high-throughput distributed systems — directly matching the core technical demands of the role — while consistently demonstrating engineering discipline, observability focus, and leadership in cultivating high-performing teams, making her a compelling fit despite gaps in fintech-specific domain exposure.
